title: Gujarati Script & Vowels
description: Discover the beautiful Gujarati script and master all 13 vowels with sounds and real examples.

blocks.title: Welcome to Gujarati! ગુજરાતીમાં સ્વાગત!
blocks.body:
Gujarati (ગુજરાતી) is an Indo-Aryan language spoken by over 60 million people, primarily in Gujarat, India. It has its own unique script — a beautiful cursive system descended from ancient Devanagari.

Good news: The script is very regular — once you learn the letters, you can read anything!

blocks.title: What You'll Learn This Week
points: The 13 Gujarati vowels (સ્વર) and their sounds
How vowels attach to consonants as diacritics (maatra)
Basic greeting words using only vowels
Your first 25 essential Gujarati vocabulary words

blocks.title: The 13 Vowels — સ્વર (Svar)
blocks.body:
Gujarati has 13 independent vowels. Each has a standalone form and a diacritic (maatra) form.

અ (a) — short 'a' like in 'about'
આ (aa) — long 'aa' like in 'father'
ઇ (i) — short 'i' like in 'bit'
ઈ (ee) — long 'ee' like in 'feet'
ઉ (u) — short 'u' like in 'put'
ઊ (oo) — long 'oo' like in 'food'
ઋ (ri) — trilled 'r' + 'i'
એ (e) — 'e' like in 'café'
ઐ (ai) — like 'ai' in 'aisle'
ઓ (o) — 'o' like in 'go'
ઔ (au) — 'ou' like in 'out'
અં (am) — nasal 'an' sound
અઃ (ah) — aspirated 'ah' sound

blocks.title: SOV — Gujarati Sentence Order
blocks.note: Unlike English (Subject-Verb-Object), Gujarati puts the verb LAST.

blocks.title: Quick Check — Fill in the Blanks

blocks.body: 💡 Pro Tip: Every consonant in Gujarati has an inherent 'a' sound. So ક alone is 'ka'. To remove the vowel, a special sign called viraam (્) is used at the end of words.

blocks.title: The Spirit of Namaste
blocks.content: In Gujarati culture, 'Namaste' (નમસ્તે) is used for BOTH hello AND goodbye — the Sanskrit origin means 'I bow to you.' The palms-together gesture (pranam) shows deep respect. You'll use this many times every day in Gujarat!

blocks.title: Week 1 Lesson — Can You Do This?
items: Say please, thank you, and sorry
Introduce yourself and ask someone's name
Understand that Gujarati uses SOV word order
Recognize the 13 Gujarati vowels by sight
